108.status: Exited in the fifth inning on May 6 after injured himself while trying to beat out an infield single in the fourth. Had trouble putting weight on his foot so he's undergoing additional testing.

Threw on April 24 in Arizona and felt good. Pitched four simulated innings with 60 pitches on April 30. Threw five innings in an Arizona Complex League game on May 5 and is nearing a rehab assignment.

Exited a Minor League rehab start on April 13 with hamstring discomfort. Struck out seven over 3 1/3 scoreless innings in a rehab start with Single-A Rancho Cucamonga on May 3 and will start with Triple-A Salt Lake on May 7.
Left his outing before the start of the third inning on April 29. Underwent an MRI, will be shut down for 3-4 weeks before starting a throwing program.
Removed from April 25 game after taking a foul tip off his left wrist. Progressed to playing catch, throwing to bases and taking light swings in the cages.
Began a rehab assignment with Single-A Rancho Cucamonga on April 29 by throwing a scoreless inning. Threw another scoreless inning with the Quakes on May 2 and allowed one run in one inning on May 5.
Was evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister in Texas and underwent season-ending ligament and flexor tendon repair surgery, the club announced April 8.
Not going to play in 2026 and had the last year of his contract deferred over several years. Was placed on 60-day IL on March 24.

110.status: After testing, received platelet-rich plasma injection on Feb. 20. Shut down from baseball activities on May 4 after experiencing discomfort during throwing progression.
Imaging came back clean. Taking anti-inflammatory medication. Began treatment May 4 and will be re-evaluated for throwing later in the week.
Dealing with a flu that has circulated around Orioles' clubhouse. Should be ready to go at end of IL stint. Joining team in Miami on May 5.
Scheduled to hit live batting practice with Double-A Chesapeake on May 5. If all goes well, will begin third rehab assignment there later in the week.
Underwent imaging on March 23 that revealed low-grade strain. Doing full baseball activities in Sarasota, Fla., as of April 24. Getting close to Minor League rehab assignment.
Injury was sustained in days after April 18 start in Cleveland. Expected to miss at least several weeks.
Got second opinion with Dr. Neal ElAttrache in Los Angeles on April 20. Has inflammation in shoulder joint. Felt soreness coming out of March 31 outing.
Exited April 11 game vs. Giants after stumbling between first and second and sliding awkwardly into the bag on a double. Broke fourth metatarsal on left foot. Expected to miss substantial time.
Exited 2026 debut on March 31 due to right elbow discomfort. After getting imaging and a second opinion, underwent Tommy John surgery on April 8.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3, tossing 25 pitches from 45 feet on flat ground.
Placed on the 60-day injured list on Feb. 14. No clear timeline for potential return.

111.status: Second Minors rehab appearance -- this one for Triple-A Worcester on May 6 -- was impressive, including high velocity and two strikeouts in one inning. There's a chance the righty's next game will be for Red Sox.
Has resumed throwing and there's a chance he'll avoid an IL stint and start on May 9 against the Rays.
Crochet has resumed playing catch, and continues to spend a lot of time in the trainer's room strengthening the shoulder.

111.expectedReturn: First day he is eligible to return on May 17
111.status: Has been dealing with neck stiffness that has limited his ability to look directly at plate when pitching. Should be fine once IL status is up.
Underwent additional testing after being recalled from his Minor League rehab assignment on April 20, and imaging revealed nothing alarming.

111.injury: Ruptured left patellar tendon/left abdominal strain
111.status: Rehab from left knee surgery went well, but now has left abdominal strain that will prolong return to action.
Imaging on right elbow revealed no structural damage. Waiting for flexor strain irritation to lessen before he can resume throwing.
Consultation with Dr. Keith Meister revealed right flexor strain. Shut down from throwing for six weeks. Transferred to 60-day IL on April 14.

111.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament right elbow/right flexor pronator strain
111.status: Started gradual throwing program on flat ground in the second week of Spring Training.

112.status: Injured left knee in fluke accident at home on May 6. Surgery is upcoming, but specifics on procedure and timetable are TBA, per manager Craig Counsell.
Removed in ninth inning on April 23. Bullpen session on May 5 went well. Has a higher-intensity session planned for May 8, per manager Craig Counsell.
Has resumed a throwing program, including a light mound workout on May 5. Cubs manager Craig Counsell said another bullpen session is planned for May 8.
Injured hand while working out April 13. Worked one scoreless inning on May 3 in fourth rehab outing with Triple-A Iowa.
Threw 75 pitches in 3 2/3 innings (six runs allowed) in fourth rehab outing with Triple-A Iowa on May 3.

Felt discomfort in recent bullpen session. Checkup with Dr. Keith Meister (who did Steele's UCL revision repair on April 18, 2025) showed a flexor strain. Shut down from throwing until asymptomatic, and will be rechecked in a month.
Felt some discomfort in April 23 outing and did not recover well. Further testing showed a flexor strain; timetable estimated to be around eight weeks.

112.playerName: Jaxon Wiggins (Cubs' No. 1 prospect, No. 50 overall)
112.status: Last pitched on April 4 for Iowa. Moved to Minor League IL out of caution. Has resumed playing catch, per president of baseball operations Jed Hoyer.
Underwent reconstruction of ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace April 20 via Dr. Keith Meister. Initial timetable for return is 12-14 months.
Underwent a right UCL revision repair with an internal brace on April 16. Timetable for return is estimated around 15-16 months.
Underwent a right patellar tendon debridement on Feb. 24. Could miss months while recovering.
Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 16 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.

113.status: Pagán grabbed his hamstring after throwing his first pitch of the night vs. the Cubs on May 5. MRI imaging showed a Grade 2 strain, with estimated time away to be four to eight weeks.
Moved to the 60-day IL on May 6 but there was no setback. Williamson received a cortisone injection after MRI showed no structural damage.
After two games for Double-A Chattanooga, Ferguson's rehab assignment moved to Triple-A Louisville on May 5.
Stretched on the field with the team and took grounders during batting practice. Also took soft tosses while hitting in the indoor cage on May 4.
Threw 79 pitches over 4 1/3 innings on rehab assignment with Triple-A Louisville on May 2. Targeting season debut on May 8 vs. Astros at Great American Ball Park.
Has begun a throwing program, and his first bullpen session could happen in late May. Greene had arthroscopic surgery March 11 to remove bone chips and loose bodies from elbow.

114.status: Felt issue while warming up in bullpen on April 24. Expected to be a minimum IL stint. Eligible to return on May 8.
Made sixth appearance of rehab assignment on April 24 with Triple-A Columbus
Suffered injury running to second base while hitting a double in fifth inning on April 6. Imaging revealed moderate strain. Expected to miss four to eight weeks.

115.status: Threw bullpen on May 5 after playing long toss on May 2. Manager Warren Schaeffer said on May 6 that Feltner would likely go on a rehab assignment.
Allowed two runs on three hits with two strikeouts in one inning for High-A Spokane in his first injury rehab appearance on April 29.

115.expectedReturn: To be determined after he is cleared for game action
115.status: Continues playing catch at the team's facility in Scottsdale, Ariz.

Underwent ligament transfer Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on March 4.
There is no return in sight for Bryant, who has had a long and painful time since signing with the Rockies before the 2022 season.

116.status: Left May 2 game with discomfort after sliding into home plate. Tests showed no structural damage, but he is still feeling some soreness.
Threw latest bullpen May 6 at close to full intensity, topping out at 94 mph on fastball. Could face hitters over the weekend, but will need a rehab start or simulated game after that.
Will undergo surgery on May 6 to remove loose bodies.

Tossed 1 2/3 innings with two strikeouts in first rehab start for Single-A Lakeland on May 3. Fastball sat at 95-96 mph, topped out at 98-99. Scheduled to make another rehab start for Lakeland on May 8.
Felt discomfort after last outing April 26, with no improvement after five days off. Undergoing further evaluation after initial tests showed no structural damage.
Threw latest bullpen session on May 5.
Scheduled to make rehab appearance for Single-A Lakeland on May 5.
Threw his first bullpen session on May 5.
Sent to Single-A Lakeland on rehab assignment on April 30.
Exited his April 28 start in the third inning. Reported gradual discomfort that did not improve between innings. The injury was diagnosed as a Grade 1 right adductor strain.
Completing rehab daily after receiving a cortisone injection in mid-April.

116.injury: Concussion, fractured left radius bone in arm
116.status: Completing rehab daily after undergoing surgery on April 13 to repair radial fracture in left forearm following an outfield collision on April 9.

117.status: Will miss the entire season after suffering a tendon injury while taking batting practice May 5. He will have surgery
Will return to Houston rotation during May 11-14 series against Seattle after giving up five walks, one run and one hit and striking out three in a Triple-A rehab start May 5.
Will continue to work out with the Major League team for the May 8-10 series at Cincinnati before going out on a Minor League rehab assignment.
Trammell is running, hitting and throwing and could be nearing a Minor League rehab assignment.
Was scratched prior to the May 4 game against the Dodgers after suffering an injury swinging the bat in BP and placed on IL.
In first rehab outing May 5 for Triple-A Sugar Land, tossed a scoreless inning, allowing one walk with a strikeout and hitting 94.7 mph.
In first Minor League rehab appearance May 5 for Triple-A Sugar Land, threw two-thirds of an inning and allowed three runs (one earned) with three walks and two strikeouts (34 pitches).
Has started his throwing program by playing catch up to 120 feet. Could throw in the bullpen next weekend.
Performing light baseball activities as he prepares to start a running program.
Has begun light baseball activity after exiting the game against the Rockies on April 8 mid-plate appearance following a swing.

117.status: Has progressed to throwing in the bullpen in West Palm Beach after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament in his right elbow on May 23, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in left elbow
117.status: Underwent Tommy John surgery on Sept. 23, 2025.

118.status: Exited May 6 start against the Guardians after three scoreless innings and 58 pitches. Undergoing further assessment.
Began rehab assignment with Triple-A Omaha on May 6, but exited outing with right shoulder discomfort. Will undergo further assessment to determine severity and next steps.
Threw 26 pitches (14 strikes) in his seventh rehab appearance with Triple-A Omaha on May 6, pitching a scoreless inning with two strikeouts and a walk.
Scratched from May 5 start against Guardians but not dealing with serious injury. Doesn't expect to miss more than one start.
Underwent left labral repair surgery on April 28, ending his season.
After missing 2025 season with an elbow injury, has continued to have problems with soreness. Scheduled to see a specialist to determine next steps.
After missing 2025 with shoulder issues, underwent right labral surgery in October. Rehabbing in Surprise, Ariz., during Spring Training but likely out for year.

119.status: Glasnow exited his May 6 start after one inning with lower back pain. He will undergo an MRI, but manager Dave Roberts said he doesn't think Glasnow will go on the IL.

Graterol began a Minor League rehab assignment on May 2. Manager Dave Roberts noted that Graterol was fine following his initial work, but that he's still got some time before he is ready to be activated.
Working on his bat speed and is scheduled to face live pitching in a simulated game on May 7, with possible rehab assignment to follow.
Tweaked his side while throwing in the bullpen on March 13. He will not begin a throwing program until he's pain-free.
Had surgery on his elbow on April 22. He thinks he will be able to start playing catch a couple weeks after the stitches are removed from his elbow.
Felt discomfort in his shoulder after his outing on April 12.
Imaging revealed inflammation, but not structural damage, in his shoulder. Has started a throwing program and essentially has an entire Spring Training ramp-up ahead.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025 and could be a second-half option for the Dodgers.
Progressed to throwing off the mound by the end of Spring Training, but the team is building him up slowly.
Started throwing full bullpen sessions in mid-March.

120.status: Signed to a one-year Major League deal with a club option for the 2027 season on May 5, but likely won't be cleared to play for at least another month.

Played catch on May 2, will continue throwing progression at Nationals' training complex.

121.injury: Left lat surgery, left hip discomfort
121.status: Was pulled off his rehab assignment May 6 after complaining of left hip discomfort. Will refrain from throwing for several days before restarting his progression, which will include a fresh 30-day rehab clock.
Restarted a throwing program May 6, nine days after receiving an epidural. Had been experiencing back discomfort since Spring Training, but did not report it until after an April 26 start.
Developed a blister during an April 13 appearance in Los Angeles. Started a rehab assignment on April 25 and has made four appearances.
Fractured his thumb sliding into first base in a May 2 loss to the Angels. Will miss 6-8 weeks.

Wrist soreness popped up April 14 in addition to his Achilles discomfort. Has been performing light baseball activities, including batting practice on the field, but remains in a week-to-week progression.
Received an injection April 30 and will rest for seven to 10 days. He will be re-evaluated then to determine his potential return date.
Strained calf April 22. Will be in a boot for a week and undergo a follow-up MRI the week of May 11. Will remain on a fluid timeline until he receives results.
Began feeling knee discomfort during an April 12 game against the Athletics. An April 14 MRI revealed a meniscus tear, requiring surgery. Had stitches removed the week of April 20.
Exited the Mets' March 21 game after four innings due to knee discomfort and underwent an MRI, which revealed the tear. Will spend his recovery on the Minor League IL.
Fractured a rib during a March 5 game against the Nationals. Will need 4-8 weeks to recover, after which he can commence his buildup.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2027.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.

133.injury: Mid foot bone bruise in right foot
133.status: Has begun a ground-based running progression and hitting progression. No timetable for return.
Still being held out of all baseball activities at this time. No timetable for return.
Began a throwing progression April 19. No timetable for his return.

134.status: Allowed one run on five hits in three innings in a rehab start May 6 for Triple-A Indianapolis. Fastball touched 99 mph. Threw 54 pitches, walking one and striking out two.

135.status: Experienced concussion symptoms after taking a fastball off the chin on April 18. Initially cleared protocols, but symptoms arose in the days afterward.
Landed on the IL after a poor start on May 1. Will take several days to rest and recover before beginning a throwing program.
Underwent surgery in October 2024. Was hopeful for an early season return but experienced a setback after making one start in Spring Training. Has played catch but has yet to throw off the mound.
Only appeared in five Cactus League games after sustaining the injury in early March. Began a rehab assignment with Triple-A El Paso on May 1.

Exited April 12 start with elbow stiffness; shut down from throwing. MRI revealed flexor strain. Expected to pitch again this season, though manager Craig Stammen noted Pivetta's absence would last "weeks and maybe months."
Underwent surgery to repair the flexor tendon in his right elbow on March 17 and will miss the 2026 season.

Underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. Will miss the 2026 season and was placed on the restricted list on March 25.
Underwent UCL internal brace surgery as well as a flexor tendon repair operation in April 2025. Resumed throwing bullpens in Padres camp.

136.status: Played catch for the first time since the issue first surfaced, on May 5. MRI revealed "trace inflammation" but no structural damage.More »
Began two-game rehab assignment at Double-A Arkansas from on May 5, going 1-for-3 with one double and two walks. Will be activated on May 8 at White Sox at start of next road trip, if all goes well.
Placed on injured list on May 4, five days after his most recent appearance.
Began a rehab assignment at Triple-A Tacoma on May 3, which will at minimum last seven days. Final hurdle to clear is throwing to the plate, along with live at-bats.
Placed on the IL before May 2 game vs. Royals.
Will make fourth rehab start on May 6 at High-A Everett, in range of 65-70 pitches. Club will assess his status after, and whether he needs a fifth outing or is ready for activation.
Injured the other (left) groin during a rehab assignment and was shut down on April 18. Will be reassessed in mid-to-late May to begin a fresh rehab assignment.
Experienced setback during early stages of throwing program and will now have to begin fresh once he's cleared to throw again.
Slated to begin rehab assignment at Triple-A Tacoma the weekend of May 2-3. Suffered the injury during brief callup from April 14-16 in San Diego.
Underwent surgery to repair his right ulnar collateral ligament in January, and will be out all season.More »

137.status: Out of lineup May 6 but available off the bench.
Exited May 5 start after throwing only 62 pitches.
Was expected to begin a rehab assignment with Triple-A Sacramento on May 5.
High-leverage reliever and sometimes closer had a recurrence of the back injury that hampered him in Spring Training. Appeared in 12 games and had two saves in three attempts.
Expected to miss six to eight weeks after undergoing surgery to remove a broken hamate from his wrist on April 16.
Has started his hitting program and is expected to resume throwing later this week.
Gave up two runs over 4 2/3 innings in his first five rehab appearances with Triple-A Sacramento.
Underwent surgery to repair the anterior capsule in his right shoulder in May 2025. Threw a live batting practice session in Arizona on April 28.
Underwent a second surgery to repair an axillary artery in his upper right arm on April 17, which will sideline him for five to six months.
Began a throwing program on April 6 and is currently tossing bullpens in Arizona.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with Dr. Keith Meister on March 25.
Expected to miss 12-14 months after undergoing Tommy John surgery on Sept. 24, 2025. Was slated to begin a throwing progression in mid-March.
Expected to miss the entire 2026 season after undergoing Tommy John surgery in November 2025.

138.status: Hit on the left leg by a 97.5 mph fastball from Brandon Sproat on May 6. Stayed in the game for another inning before being replaced defensively.
Manager Oliver Marmol described that Urías has dealt with elbow discomfort on and off dating back to spring. Team will shut him down for multiple weeks before re-evaluating.
Pitched a scoreless inning vs. Indianapolis on May 3 as he continues a rehab assignment with Triple-A Memphis.
Expected to begin rehab assignment around May 10, per Cardinals manager Oliver Marmol. Eligible to come off injured list May 24.

139.status: Faced hitters in live BP April 30, threw a bullpen session May 3 and was scheduled to start a rehab assignment with Triple-A. Returned to the Trop on May 6, however, with the expectation that he will rejoin the big league staff in a multi-inning role in Boston.

139.injury: Right shoulder impingement and ankle sprain
139.status: Sprained ankle running to first base April 11 on rehab assignment. Resumed assignment with Triple-A Durham on April 24. Has been playing regularly and could make his Rays debut in Boston.

Felt soreness in the sixth inning of his May 3 start, then underwent an MRI on May 4 that revealed inflammation. Imaging showed no structural issues, so he should resume throwing after a brief period of rest.
Signed to a Major League contract on April 14 and immediately placed on the IL. Scheduled to face hitters for the first time the weekend of May 8-10, with a rehab assignment to follow.

Imaging revealed no structural damage. Faced hitters in live BP April 30, threw a bullpen session May 3 and departed for Triple-A Durham to begin a rehab assignment. Will need time to get built up as a starter.
Did not pitch all spring while dealing with a back issue that required an injection. Also underwent an appendectomy. Will need time to build up after being shut down so long.

Set for season-ending surgery on May 13. Said multiple exams revealed "normal" wear and tear on labrum and femoroacetabular impingement, which limited his leg's range of motion. Expecting a six-month recovery followed by a full offseason and plenty of time to be ready for Spring Training.
Sustained a Grade 2 left ankle sprain while running the bases for Triple-A Durham on April 22. Imaging revealed no fracture.

After rocky rehab outing on April 15, he reported soreness in back of right shoulder. Further evaluation revealed a new injury, a subscapularis strain. Shut down for 2-3 weeks, meaning he's likely 4-6 weeks from returning.
Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8. Hopes to return in June or July.

140.status: Smith played all nine innings on May 3 against the Tigers. Ezequiel Duran will likely get the bulk of the time at second base while Smith is out.
Langford flew from Arizona to join the team in Detroit on May 2, and will not begin ramping up his swing for at least a week, focusing on cardio work and conditioning until cleared to hit.

Had another setback in throwing program. Will continue to be no-throw for at least another few days. Hopes to throw plyo balls early next week. Threw 27 pitches in rehab appearance with Triple-A Round Rock April 2. Has not pitched since.
Was up to 97 mph in one inning of a rehab appearance with Triple-A Round Rock on April 23. Will need to throw on back-to-back days before he returns to the big league club.
Examined by team doctors on April 20 for shoulder soreness and was placed on the IL on April 23.
Only threw one inning in an April 14 loss to the A’s due to arm fatigue that was eventually classified as shoulder impingement.
Left Tuesday night’s loss to the A’s after throwing two pitches due to feeling something in his arm. Scans showed minimal issues, though he will be down for at least two weeks.
Down four to six weeks minimum before re-evaluation.
Signed to a Major League deal on Feb. 13, though is continuing to rehab from Tommy John surgery. Should be back on a big league mound around the Trade Deadline.

141.status: Made latest rehab start with Triple-A Buffalo on May 3, allowing seven earned runs over 3 2/3 innings and displaying decreased velocity. Felt more sore than usual afterwards, so he underwent MRI on May 5. Team expects to know more on his status by May 7.

141.status: Resumed activity by playing light catch at Tropicana Field on May 4 and 6, but said his forearm isn't responding as desired. Will meet with doctors to discuss next steps. Said his ankle discomfort has lessened.
Threw a "two-up" side session with no issues on May 6 and will throw another next week. Will start facing hitters after that.
Has started throwing and will start hitting in the cage this weekend. Has not begun catching, but he is progressing on schedule.
Has been rehabbing at Florida complex. Started running on May 5 and is about 10 days away from beginning a rehab assignment.
Expected to pitch in rehab games with FCL Blue Jays beginning on May 8 as he continues to build up in Dunedin, Fla.
Remains in Florida. Manager John Schneider did not have an immediate update on his throwing status but mentioned he would be checking on it.
Discovered after Estrada was optioned, so he has been placed on the Major League IL.
Underwent surgery and is expected to miss the rest of 2026.
Underwent surgery to repair the labral tear and is hoping to return to the Blue Jays in five to six months. This timeline should be clearer by June, but any return in 2026 would be a success.
Underwent surgery and will miss the entire 2026 season.

Miller is fielding ground balls. “He’s doing everything except for swinging,” manager Rob Thomson said. “We’re hoping to get that going this week.” Miller’s progress has been slow and the Phillies have declined to delve into details about it.

Will miss the 2026 season after tearing his right ACL during a collision at first base during a Grapefruit League game on March 8.
Underwent arthroscopic surgery to remove bone spurs from his elbow on Feb. 18. There is a chance he could return in June or July, but the team isn’t putting a timetable on it.
Missed 2025 while recovering from left knee surgery and he had a cleanup procedure in November. There is no timetable for his return.

145.status: Continues with the bullpen portion of his recovery ramp-up, having thrown seven. Has felt great as the rehab process moves forward.

The White Sox Opening Day starter left his last start for Triple-A Charlotte on April 30 due to the shoulder issue. It’s the first time Smith has dealt with this malady this season.
Manager Will Venable wasn’t sure when Pereira suffered the injury during an April 28 win over the Angels. But it was bothering Pereira both hitting and throwing.
Making progress and getting better every day, as he told MLB.com on April 29. But there’s still no set timetable for an injury rehab assignment.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with the procedure performed by Dr. Keith Meister, but was with the team for the home opener.
Placed on 60-day IL as he continues to recover from Tommy John surgery he underwent last year. Has thrown numerous times off the mound.
Underwent successful surgery to repair the torn ACL on Oct. 28. Estimated recovery time is eight months, per the team.

146.status: Threw 13 pitches off mound on May 6. Will throw again on May 8. Exited April 27 outing after experiencing diminished sensation in pitching hand.

146.playerName: Kevin Defrank (Marlins No. 5 prospect, No. 100 overall)
146.status: No surgery required but also no timeline for a return.

According to manager Clayton McCullough, Conine is running at 70 percent, is hitting and throwing. Trending toward a quicker timeline of return. Underwent left hamstring tendon excision week of April 13.

Left the May 3 game against the Orioles after hurting his hand on a low pickoff attempt. X-rays were negative. Was out of the lineup on May 4 and 5. Scheduled to attempt hitting on May 5.

Made third rehab start on April 29, throwing 5 2/3 innings for Double-A Somerset. Scheduled to make fourth rehab start on May 5 for High-A Hudson Valley. Still "a ways away" from MLB activation, Aaron Boone said May 5.

Left the April 24 game in Houston for a pinch-runner after feeling tightness in calf. Underwent MRI on April 27. Placed on injured list April 28, retroactive to April 25. Has not resumed running as of May 5.

Threw 10 pitches from a mound April 10, his first time doing so since Tommy John surgery in July 2025. Could face hitters in June and is on track for MLB activation sometime in August.

Was a late scratch from the lineup on May 6 at St. Louis. (last updated: May 6)
Scheduled for season-ending Tommy John surgery on May 11. (last updated: May 6)
